old_claim.note: Compass-Based Sea Routes provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Sternpost Rudders and Compass-Based Sea Routes; Sternpost rudders are steering hardware and do not depend on compass-planned sea-route systems.

source_shape.source_locator: Article states that the sternpost rudder, hinged at its forward edge, became common after the 11th century.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Sternpost rudders are steering hardware and do not depend on compass-planned sea-route systems.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Sternpost Rudders claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Compass-Based Sea Routes as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Sternpost Rudders treated Compass-Based Sea Routes as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Sternpost Rudders no longer directly depends on Compass-Based Sea Routes; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: ship_rudders_sternpost must not directly depend on marine_compass_routes unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows ship_rudders_sternpost is intentionally scoped only to cases that require marine_compass_routes.
The graph splits ship_rudders_sternpost and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on marine_compass_routes.
A stronger source demonstrates that marine_compass_routes is chronologically and causally necessary for ship_rudders_sternpost.
short_reason: Sternpost rudders are steering hardware and do not depend on compass-planned sea-route systems.
